title: QA: PermissionManager logs decisions at debug level; audit trail invisible in production
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/permissions.rs:172-226, 254-262
## Violation
All permission decisions log at `debug!` level:
```rust
debug!(
    tool = %canonical_tool, target = %target, pattern = %rule.pattern,
    "Allowed by persisted always-allow rule"
);
...
debug!("Allowed by session rule");
...
debug!("Allowed by default_allow config pattern");
```
Production deployments typically run at `info` or `warn`. The result:
**every successful permission grant is invisible**. There is no
default-visible audit trail of which tool calls were allowed, by which
rule, against which target. OWASP A09 — security logging failure for
authorization decisions. Only `warn!` (malformed args, sandbox bypass
attempt) reaches default-level logs.
## Mandated refactor
Promote `Allowed by ...` decisions to `info!` (or `audit!` if a
dedicated channel exists) so production logs always capture
authorization outcomes. Emit one structured field per decision: tool,
target, decision (allow/deny/prompt), rule_tier (persisted/session/
default_allow/safety), and pattern. Consider routing security-relevant
logs to a separate sink (file or syslog) so they survive log rotation
or app-level log-level changes.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
